Meridian Corp recently expanded its primary operational headquarters into central Frankfurt.
The release of the new CyberShield product line represented the largest product launch in Meridian Corp's history.
Meridian Corp successfully acquired AlphaLogistics in Q2 2025 to bolster its supply chain capabilities.
The board of directors appointed Sarah Jenkins as the new Chief Technology Officer in late 2024.
Meridian Corp shifted its entire European server infrastructure to 100% renewable energy.
The company experienced a temporary 2-hour outage in its US-East operational zone during July 2025.
Meridian Corp was named 'Most Innovating Supply Chain Software' by the Global Tech Logistics Council.
Following the AlphaLogistics acquisition, the company retired its legacy tracking platform in favor of the new unified system.
CEO Mark Leston announced a shift toward AI-driven logistics routing at the 2025 Annual Stakeholder Summit.
Meridian Corp faced localized strikes in its South American delivery hubs during early 2025.
A multi-year strategic partnership was formed between Meridian Corp and Quantum Compute Inc.
Meridian Corp launched a comprehensive retraining program to upskill warehouse workers in robotics.
For three consecutive quarters, Meridian Corp held the largest market share in automated inventory management.
The company divested its consumer hardware division to focus entirely on enterprise B2B SaaS solutions.
Meridian Corp established a dedicated sustainability committee answering directly to the CEO.
Operations in the APAC region grew to become the company's second-largest revenue driver by 2025.
An internal reorganization merged the data science and product engineering departments into a single entity.
Meridian Corp successfully defended against a highly publicized cyber-attack in late 2024 with no data loss.
The company implemented a hybrid 3-day work-from-office policy globally in 2025.
A new class of restricted stock units (RSUs) was introduced to incentivize long-term employee retention.
Meridian Corp opened its first wholly-owned robotics manufacturing facility in Texas.
The company was forced to recall batch #A8 of its tracking beacons due to a faulty battery vendor.
Meridian Corp pledged to reduce its carbon footprint to net-zero by the year 2035.
Regulatory headwinds in the European Union delayed the launch of the predictive forecasting module by six months.
The acquisition of BetaRoute was blocked by antitrust regulators in the UK.
Meridian Corp upgraded its core cloud infrastructure provider, migrating 500 databases in a single weekend.
The company launched an open-source initiative for its internal microservices framework.
A class-action lawsuit filed by independent contractors was settled favorably out of court.
Meridian Corp received the ISO 27001 certification for its revised data handling policies.
The corporate headquarters in Seattle was renovated to include a 50,000 square foot research laboratory.
